question: What did Nephi teach about faith?
answer: Nephi's the one I lean on for faith — he kept moving even when the trip was awful, and he keeps saying God will prepare a way (1 Nephi 3:7, 1 Nephi 7:12). 2 Nephi 31 is where he lines up faith with repentance, baptism, and the Holy Ghost as the actual path. Faith for him isn't just vibes — it's trusting enough to obey. 2 Nephi 31:19–20 ties that to enduring to the end.

question: What did Alma the Elder teach about baptism?
answer: After Abinadi got to him, Alma the Elder started the church at the Waters of Mormon. The baptism language he uses is Mosiah 18:8–10 — bear each other's burdens, mourn with people, stand as witnesses, get cleansed through Christ. Mosiah 18:13 and 16–17 talk about what happened to the people who said yes. So baptism isn't just you and God alone — it's joining a community that actually shows up for each other.

question: What did Alma the Younger teach about repentance?
answer: Alma the Younger's whole arc is basically the repentance book — from troublemaker to missionary/judge after the angel thing (Alma 36). he says real repentance is a mighty change of heart (Alma 5:14) and he cares a lot about personal revelation (Alma 5:45–46). Alma 42 on justice and mercy is where he explains how repentance fits God's law without pretending sin doesn't matter.

question: What did King Benjamin teach about the Holy Ghost?
answer: Benjamin's people got a mighty change and a remission of sins (Mosiah 4:2–3), which lines up with what the Spirit does for people who covenant with Christ. Mosiah 5:2 says the Spirit changed their hearts. he's filed under repentance in my app, but his talk sets people up to receive the Spirit — natural man stuff (Mosiah 3:19), service (Mosiah 2:17), all that.

question: What did Moroni teach about the Gift of the Holy Ghost?
answer: Moroni connects the gift to real church life — Moroni 6:4 says members get nourished by the Spirit after baptism. Moroni 8:25–26 strings faith, repentance, baptism, and the gift together. Moroni 10:4–5 is the promise to ask God and learn by the Spirit. Ether 12:27 (in his part of the record) about grace and weakness fits that same theme for me.

question: What does enduring to the end mean in the Book of Mormon?
answer: Enduring to the end is staying on the covenant path — faith, repentance, baptism, Holy Ghost — for the long haul, like Nephi says in 2 Nephi 31:19–20. It's not just surviving; it's sticking with Christ. Mormon (who I put under this pillar) wrote Moroni 7 about charity and faith while everything was collapsing — endurance when the world fails, not when it's easy.

question: Who best exemplifies baptism?
answer: I picked Alma the Elder for baptism — he actually organized the church at the Waters of Mormon and taught Mosiah 18:8–10. Other people teach doctrine that leads to baptism, but he's the one who models giving baptism and building a covenant people around it.

question: What is the Doctrine of Christ?
answer: It's the path Nephi lays out in 2 Nephi 31 — faith in Christ, repentance, baptism, gift of the Holy Ghost, enduring to the end. This app just splits those into five "pillars" and hooks each one to verses and prophets. Together it's supposed to describe how we come to Christ and stay with him.

question: What did Abinadi teach?
answer: Abinadi testified in front of Noah's court about Christ and the resurrection — wouldn't take it back even when it cost his life (Mosiah 13:28; Mosiah 15; Mosiah 16:14–15). he taught about the Godhead and the Atonement in a way that cracked Alma the Elder open. his words are what started the church at Mormon. Faith over safety, basically.

question: What did Amulek say about repentance?
answer: Alma 34 — infinite, eternal Atonement (Alma 34:14–16) and don't sleep on repentance (Alma 34:31–33). he keeps saying this life is when you prepare to meet God. Alma 11:40 names Christ as Redeemer. And his own life — walking away from money and status — shows what repentance can cost and why it's worth it.

question: How does Nephi show us how to endure to the end?
answer: I listed enduring as one of his secondary pillars alongside faith — his life was decades of obeying when family was messy and travel was brutal. 2 Nephi 31:19–20 says enduring to the end is part of the whole Doctrine of Christ, not an extra add-on. Verses like 1 Nephi 3:7 and 2 Nephi 31:19–20 are how I try to picture endurance — not one heroic day, but keeping at it.

question: What did Mormon teach?
answer: Mormon commanded armies and abridged the plates while his people self-destructed. Moroni 7 is huge — faith, hope, charity, telling good from evil (Moroni 7:33, 47–48). he keeps teaching clearly even when politically and militarily it's all going wrong. That's why I put him under enduring.

question: What did Jacob teach about repentance?
answer: Jacob was a priest/teacher — Jacob 2 in the temple doesn't dance around sin; he calls it out but still points people to Christ. Jacob 1:7 says cling to God; Jacob 4:11–12 centers Christ as the one who atones. Repentance here starts with hearing hard truth in a sacred place and actually turning back.

question: Who are the main prophets in the Book of Mormon?
answer: This explorer only highlights a handful of voices I tied to pillars — Nephi, Lehi, Jacob, King Benjamin, Abinadi, Alma the Elder, Alma the Younger, Amulek, Mormon, Moroni, plus a few more in the dataset. Not every prophet in the book, just ones that fit my project for teaching the Doctrine of Christ. There are tons of others I didn't include.

question: What verses talk about faith?
answer: Faith shows up everywhere; here I anchored it through specific people — Nephi (1 Nephi 3:7, 7:12), Abinadi (Mosiah 13–16), Amulek (Alma 11:40, Alma 34), Mormon (Moroni 7), etc. The Faith pillar's main verse is 2 Nephi 31:19. If you click through each person's key verses in the explorer, that's my starter list — not the only verses in the whole book.

question: What is the significance of Mosiah 18?
answer: That's the Waters of Mormon — Alma baptizing people after they ran from Noah. Verses 8–10 spell out what baptism means in practice, and 13 / 16–17 describe the joy after. It's the story Nephites keep pointing back to for what covenant baptism and church fellowship looked like.

question: What did Lehi teach?
answer: Lehi saw visions, warned Jerusalem, and taught his family in the new land. 2 Nephi 2 is his big theology chapter — agency, opposition, Christ's redemption (2 Nephi 2:6–8, 27–28). 1 Nephi 1:14–15 shows his call. I put him under Faith because he kept following revelation even when it wrecked his comfortable life.

question: How do I apply the Doctrine of Christ today?
answer: Same pattern Nephi gives — faith, repentance, living your covenants, Holy Ghost, keep going (2 Nephi 31). Day to day that looks like prayer, scripture, sacrament, serving people, repenting when you mess up — kind of like King Benjamin's people after a mighty change. Alma 32 is experimenting on the word; Moroni 10 is asking God. Ancient doctrine, modern choices.

question: What did Moroni promise?
answer: Moroni 10:4–5 — read, think about it, ask God sincerely, you can know truth by the Spirit. he also talks about the Spirit nourishing members after baptism (Moroni 6:4) and ties the steps together in Moroni 8:25–26. After everything he lived through, Ether 12:27 about grace and weakness still hits.

question: What is the baptismal covenant?
answer: Alma the Elder lays it out in Mosiah 18:8–10 — mourn with people, bear burdens, stand as witnesses of God, serve him. That's what you take on with baptism, and it connects to taking Christ's name and being spiritually reborn (like Benjamin's people). Waters of Mormon is the template I think about for what baptism commits you to toward God and other people.
